signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.48; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) vs BoJ 0.75% (ON HOLD) — 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential favors USD. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. Retail sentiment shows 11% long / 89% short (EXTREME SHORT bias). AUD/USD at 0.7161; RBA 4.10% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) — 35bp AUD carry premium supports the pair structurally. Watch for LF delta turning negative or retail sentiment shifting to invalidate the extreme positioning read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.351; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. ATM IV 9.5% near HV — options neutral.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Technical
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5909; NZD/USD vs NZX 50 corr=+0.661 (z=+2.20σ) — correlation break. Watch 0.6000 as the next level on a sustained move.
